meaning: vt.恶化,增剧,激怒,使加剧,使烦恼
examples: Noise in classrooms can only exacerbate their difficulty in comprehending and processing verbal communication with other children and instructions from the teacher.
Moreover, putting young people in prison can exacerbate the problems—a system of mentors and helpers could be provided for those who unthinkingly break the law.
Central banks in the developing world are now worried that falling currencies will exacerbate inflationary pressures.
That in turn could cause mayhem in financial markets and exacerbate the eurozone debt crisis.
